text:
tasi (Unseed) is to be determined from the flowers of the
Vetasa tree; Kadrava (a kind of grain), from the flowers of the Pafafa
tree; conch shells, pearls and silver, from the Tilaka tree; and hemp,
from the Ihgudi tree.
The welfare and price of elephants should be read from the
Hastikarpa trees; those of horses, from the Asvakarna; those of cows
from the PStala trees (trumpet flower); and those of goats and
sheep, from the banana plants.
mcm
The abundance and cheapness of gold is to be inferred from
the Campaka flowers; those of corals, from the Bandhujiva flowers;
those of diamonds from the growth of Kuravaka trees (crimson
variety of amaranth); and those of Vaidurya (beryl) from the
Nandifavarta trees.
VTW: I
Trfitcq#! TT5n 115.11
The prospects of pearls are to be ascertained from the Sindhu-
vara tree; those of artisans, from saffron; those of the king, from red
lotuses; and those of the minister, from blue lilies.

284 Brhat SamhitH
